title: Implement dispatch retry with exponential backoff
body: In src/lib/dispatch-runner.ts, add a retryDispatch(dispatchId: string, maxRetries: number = 3) function. It should: (1) read the failed dispatch JSON from the dispatches directory, (2) implement exponential backoff with jitter — base delay 1000ms, multiplier 2, max delay 30000ms, jitter ±20%, (3) reset dispatch status to 'pending' and clear error fields before each retry, (4) call runDispatch() internally, (5) after maxRetries exhausted set status to 'failed_permanent', (6) return {success: boolean, attempts: number, final_status: string, error?: string}. TypeScript strict mode, async/await, proper error handling, JSDoc comments.

title: Implement session context builder
body: Create src/lib/session-context.ts with a buildSessionContext(projectPath: string) function. It should: (1) read .claude-project JSON to get memory_path and agent configs, (2) query research.db memories table for last 20 entries ordered by ts DESC, (3) query dispatch_observations for last 10 completed dispatches with their titles and outcomes, (4) read AGENT_STATE.md if it exists and extract current_phase and overall_progress fields, (5) return a SessionContext interface with fields: memories (array), recent_dispatches (array), agent_state (object), project_config (object), built_at (ISO string). Must handle missing files gracefully. TypeScript strict, full error handling.

title: Analyse ablation v1 results and identify improvement areas
body:
You have access to the following ablation v1 summary data. Analyse it and provide actionable recommendations:

baseline: n=26, avg_input=80.6 tokens, avg_output=1043.8 tokens, latency=6840ms
cache_only: n=30, avg_input=79.4, avg_output=1052.9, cache_hit_rate=0%, latency=6677ms
format_only: n=30, avg_input=141.4 (+75%), avg_output=1028.3, latency=6747ms
clarity_only: n=30, avg_input=111.3 (+38%), avg_output=849.7 (-19% output), latency=14724ms
llmlingua_only: n=30, avg_input=79.4, avg_output=1002.3, latency=6564ms (passthrough)
pd_only: n=30, avg_input=79.4, avg_output=1050.7, latency=6744ms (empty registry)
full_system: n=30, avg_input=154.8 (+92%), avg_output=884.4 (-15% output), latency=19728ms

Key questions to address: (1) Why did clarity_only reduce output by 19% while expanding input? (2) Is the latency increase in clarity_only and full_system acceptable given quality gains? (3) What is the optimal task size threshold where format_only starts compressing rather than expanding? (4) What metrics should ablation v2 track that v1 missed? Provide specific numeric targets for each condition in v2.

title: Add pattern analyzer for automatic tool generation
body: Create src/lib/pattern-analyzer.ts that identifies repeated task patterns from dispatch history. It should: (1) query dispatch_observations for all completed dispatches grouped by task_type, (2) use simple string similarity (Jaccard on word tokens, threshold 0.6) to cluster tasks with similar bodies, (3) for clusters of size >= 3, generate a PatternDefinition: {id: string, name: string, trigger_keywords: string[], template_body: string, avg_tokens_saved: number, occurrence_count: number}, (4) export analyzePatterns(db: Database): Promise<PatternDefinition[]>, (5) export savePatterns(db: Database, patterns: PatternDefinition[]): Promise<void> that upserts to a pattern_definitions table (create if not exists). Full TypeScript, proper interfaces, error handling.

title: Write integration tests for dispatch pipeline
body: Create tests/integration/dispatch-pipeline.test.ts using the existing test framework in the project. Write integration tests that cover: (1) createDispatch() creates a valid JSON file with correct schema — test all required fields (id, title, body, status, created_at, agent), (2) runDispatch() with a mock Anthropic client returns a completed observation with token counts, (3) retryDispatch() retries on failure and respects maxRetries limit — mock the first 2 calls to fail, third to succeed, (4) getSpend() returns correct totals after 3 known dispatches are inserted into a test DB, (5) buildSystemPrompt() returns a string of at least 1000 characters containing the agent name. Use beforeEach to reset state, afterAll to clean up temp files and test DB. Mock the Anthropic SDK client — do not make real API calls in tests.

title: Build nightly pattern crystallization script
body: Create scripts/crystallize-patterns.ts as a standalone Node.js script (runnable with npx ts-node). It should: (1) connect to research.db, (2) call analyzePatterns() from pattern-analyzer.ts, (3) for each new PatternDefinition found (not yet in pattern_definitions table), generate a tool stub file at mcp/tools/auto/{pattern_id}.ts with a basic MCP tool implementation using the pattern template, (4) generate a corresponding slash command at .claude/commands/auto-{pattern_id}.md, (5) call savePatterns() to persist them, (6) output a summary: patterns_found, patterns_new, files_created, (7) store a memory entry: store summary in memories table with category='crystallization'. Must be idempotent — running twice produces same result. Full error handling, TypeScript.

title: Refactor dispatch-runner to use model config
body: Refactor src/lib/dispatch-runner.ts to fully use the model config from src/config/models.ts. Current issues to fix: (1) any remaining hardcoded model strings should use getModel() with appropriate tier, (2) add a shouldCache(model: string, estimatedInputTokens: number): boolean function that checks CACHE_MIN_TOKENS thresholds — return false if below threshold to avoid overhead, (3) the dispatch function should accept an optional tier parameter defaulting to 'dispatch', (4) when cache is skipped due to token threshold, log a debug message: 'cache skipped: {model} requires {threshold} tokens, got {actual}', (5) add estimatedCost field to the dispatch observation using estimateCost() from models.ts, (6) update the research-db insertion to include model name and estimated cost. Maintain all existing function signatures for backward compatibility.

title: Write technical architecture document
body: Write a comprehensive ARCHITECTURE.md for the claude-project repository. It must cover: (1) System overview — what the project does in 2-3 sentences, (2) Component diagram in ASCII art showing: CLI → dispatch-runner → Anthropic API, MCP server → research.db, clarity layer → Ollama, (3) TGCH Framework explanation — Token cache (T), Grammar/Clarity (G), Compression (C), Habit/PD (H) — one paragraph each with current implementation status, (4) Data flow — step by step what happens when a dispatch is created and run, (5) Database schema — describe dispatch_observations, memories, pattern_definitions tables with key columns, (6) Configuration — .claude-project fields explained, models.ts tiers, CACHE_MIN_TOKENS, (7) Known limitations — current proxy setup, Haiku cache threshold, empty pattern registry, (8) Next steps — ablation v2, iterations counter fix, LLMLingua installation. Markdown format, professional tone, 600-900 words.

title: Fix iterations counter in dispatch observations
body: The iterations field in dispatch_observations is always stored as 0. Fix this in src/lib/dispatch-runner.ts. The iterations counter should track: (1) how many times the model was called within a single dispatch run (some tasks may require multiple calls for tool use or continuation), (2) if the Anthropic response has stop_reason === 'max_tokens', that counts as a continuation — increment iterations and make a follow-up call with the partial response prepended, (3) if stop_reason === 'end_turn', iterations = 1 (single call completed), (4) update the dispatch observation record with the final iterations count before writing to research.db, (5) add a total_tokens_all_iterations field that sums tokens across all iteration calls. The effective_tokens metric (input × iterations) can then be computed correctly in the ablation report. Handle edge cases: max 5 iterations hard limit, log warning if limit reached.

title: Design the VectorBrain MCP integration plan
body: Design a detailed integration plan for connecting the VectorBrain project (Anki Vector robot AI using <robot-platform> + Ollama on Mac) to the claude-project MCP server. The plan should cover: (1) Which MCP tools VectorBrain should call — get_context() for session memory, store_memory() for robot interaction logs, dispatch_task() for complex reasoning tasks Vector can't handle locally, (2) How to handle the latency constraint — Vector's response time budget is ~500ms, MCP calls may take 2-5s, so what gets dispatched async vs handled locally by Ollama, (3) The clarity layer adaptation — Vector's voice input will be noisy and informal, how should the clarity rewrite be tuned differently for voice vs text input, (4) Memory schema for robot interactions — what categories and tags to use for Vector-specific memories, (5) A phased rollout: Phase 1 (logging only), Phase 2 (async dispatch for complex queries), Phase 3 (full bidirectional memory sync). Concrete implementation steps for each phase.
